current_status_summary: Resolving the Lean 4 syntax and logic failure in the Beta block. Synchronizing the Cut-and-Paste property across both Alpha and Beta to correctly reflect the mixed-input splicing property of two-way Turing machines.
acknowledged_critic_fallacies: Acknowledged that the second block retained the broken cut_paste_property definition, leading to a type mismatch when applying h_cross_eq. The property must strictly assert output_of_pair x1 y2 = output_of_pair x1 y1 to correctly model two-way TM splicing.
alpha_directive: Re-verify and compile the corrected CutAndPaste property: def CutAndPaste := \forall x1 y1 x2 y2, CS (x1, y1) = CS (x2, y2) \rightarrow Out (x1, y2) = Out (x1, y1). Ensure the proof fooling_set_implies_CS_injective_on_S remains syntactically valid and free of type mismatches.
beta_directive: Update cut_paste_property to \forall (x1 x2 y1 y2 : FRSB_Information_Cut k), compute_crossing x1 y1 = compute_crossing x2 y2 \rightarrow output_of_pair x1 y2 = output_of_pair x1 y1. In FRSB_CutAndPaste_Crossing_Sequence_Bound, extract a and b from the diagonal, assume h_cross_eq : compute_crossing a a = compute_crossing b b. Apply the corrected property using x1 := a, y1 := a, x2 := b, y2 := b to deduce output_of_pair a b = output_of_pair a a. Use h_correct to show false = true, completing the proof without type errors.
critic_target: Verify that Beta's updated cut_paste_property exactly matches the mixed-input splicing logic and that the proof of FRSB_CutAndPaste_Crossing_Sequence_Bound compiles without type mismatches. Ensure the 2^k lower bound on the crossing sequence cardinality is rigorously established.
